card.intuition: Syntactic sugar built on top of Promises that allows you to write asynchronous code that looks and behaves like synchronous code, making it much easier to read and maintain.
card.analogy: Reading a choose-your-own-adventure book: instead of having to write 'go to page 50 if success' (Promise), you just read the book page by page. When a page says 'Wait for 5 minutes' (await), you stop reading until time is up, then continue exactly where you left off.
gotchas: `await` blocks the execution of the current function, but it NOT block the rest of the application (it yields to the Event Loop)
If you forget the `await` keyword, you'll get back the Promise object itself instead of the data you expected

exercise.prompt: Complete the try/catch logic for an async function.
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "await"
assert check("await")
print("PASS")
